tasks.name: Project Setup & Initial Documentation
tasks.description: Set up project structure, initialize documentation files, and create core configuration
notes: Successfully created project directory structure with data, models, backtesting, utils, tests, and docs folders
Initialized todo.json with comprehensive task list for the entire project
Created AGENTS.md with project workflow, guidelines, and coding standards
Established CHANGELOG.md for tracking all significant changes
Reviewd existing data structure - confirmed forward-adjusted prices are available for return calculation
tasks.execution_summary: Project setup complete. All core documentation files are in place, and task tracking system is operational.
tasks.results: Project structure is ready for development. Data format has been verified to contain all necessary fields.
tasks.lessons_learned: Clear project structure and documentation are essential for maintaining organization throughout the development process. Starting with comprehensive task tracking helps prioritize work and maintain focus.

tasks.name: Data Loading & Preprocessing Module
tasks.description: Create module to load stock data, preprocess it, and split into train/val/test sets
notes: Use forward-adjusted prices for return calculation
Split data 8:1:1 by time
Handle missing data and feature engineering
tasks.execution_summary: Implemented comprehensive data loading and preprocessing module with all required functionality.
results: Successfully loaded and preprocessed stock data with forward-adjusted prices
Calculated daily returns as target variable (close/open - 1)
Created additional technical features including rolling statistics (mean, std) and momentum indicators
Implemented time-based train/val/test split (80/10/10)
Handled missing data through forward fill, backward fill, and dropping remaining NaNs
Data loader supports loading multiple stocks and combines them into a single DataFrame
Successfully tested with 3 sample stocks, producing clean features and target variables
lessons_learned: Time-based split is crucial for financial data to avoid look-ahead bias
Forward-adjusted prices must be used for accurate return calculations
Feature engineering should create both lagged features and rolling statistics
Handling missing data carefully is essential for model performance
Modular design makes it easy to extend functionality later

tasks.name: Base Model Interface & Sklearn Models
tasks.description: Create abstract base model class, implement sklearn regression models for daily return prediction
notes: Support multiple regression algorithms (Linear, Random Forest, XGBoost, etc.)
Implement model training, inference, and evaluation methods
tasks.execution_summary: Implemented abstract base classes for all models, and created Linear Regression and Random Forest models using scikit-learn.
results: Created BaseModel abstract class with common functionality (save/load, logging, metrics)
Created RegressionModel abstract class with regression-specific metrics and evaluation
Implemented LinearRegression model with scikit-learn, including feature scaling, training, and evaluation
Implemented RandomForest model with scikit-learn, supporting hyperparameter tuning option
Both models support comprehensive evaluation metrics including MAE, MSE, RMSE, R², Pearson/Spearman correlation
Both models support top-N performance evaluation (top 5, 10, 50, 100)
Tested models on sample data: Random Forest shows better performance with R²=0.16 on test set vs Linear Regression's R²=-0.19
Top-5 predictions from Random Forest achieve 0.039 average return with 80% win rate
lessons_learned: Random Forest outperforms Linear Regression on this task, likely due to non-linear relationships in the data
Feature scaling is not required for Random Forest but helps maintain consistency across models
Top-N evaluation is more relevant for trading strategies than traditional regression metrics
Proper abstraction through base classes allows for easy extension to other models
Model performance on validation/test sets is significantly lower than training set, indicating overfitting that needs to be addressed

tasks.name: Deep Learning Models with PyTorch
tasks.description: Implement deep learning models using PyTorch for return prediction
notes: Create feedforward neural networks, maybe LSTMs for sequential data
Implement training loops with validation, early stopping, and model saving
tasks.execution_summary: Implemented Feedforward Neural Network model using PyTorch with comprehensive training loop and validation.
results: Created flexible FeedforwardNN class with configurable hidden layers and dropout
Implemented training loop with early stopping to prevent overfitting
Added model checkpointing to save best model based on validation loss
Tested model on sample data: Test set R² = 0.139, slightly lower than Random Forest (0.159) but better than Linear Regression (-0.19)
Top-10 predictions achieve 0.0386 average return with 80% win rate, comparable to Random Forest's performance
Training history visualization automatically saved to plots directory
lessons_learned: Deep learning models require careful regularization to avoid overfitting on financial data
Early stopping is essential for preventing overfitting and improving generalization
Feedforward networks show competitive performance with tree-based models on this task
Model checkpointing allows preserving the best model state during training

tasks.name: Evaluation Metrics Module
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive evaluation metrics including correlation coefficients, top-N performance
notes: Correlation coefficient (Pearson, Spearman)
Top 5, 10, 50, 100 performance metrics
Additional metrics like MAE, MSE, R²
tasks.execution_summary: Implemented comprehensive evaluation metrics as part of the regression model base class.
results: Traditional regression metrics: MAE, MSE, RMSE, R²
Correlation metrics: Pearson correlation, Spearman correlation
Top-N performance metrics: average return, median return, win rate, vs market for top 5, 10, 50, 100 predictions
All metrics automatically calculated during model evaluation
Metrics are logged to both console and files for later analysis
lessons_learned: Top-N performance metrics are more relevant for trading strategies than traditional regression metrics
Correlation metrics provide better indication of prediction quality than R² for financial data
Comprehensive metrics allow better understanding of model strengths and weaknesses
Consistent evaluation across all models enables fair comparison

tasks.name: Backtesting Framework
tasks.description: Create backtesting module that supports custom strategies based on model predictions
notes: Support long-only strategies
Calculate key performance metrics (Sharpe ratio, max drawdown, etc.)
Allow custom strategy implementation

tasks.name: Training Logging & Experiment Tracking
tasks.description: Implement detailed training logging and experiment tracking system
notes: Log hyperparameters, training metrics, validation results
Save logs to files for later analysis
tasks.execution_summary: Implemented comprehensive logging system for tracking all model training and evaluation activities.
results: Created Logger and ModelLogger classes with flexible logging capabilities
Logs are saved to both console and rotating log files
Hyperparameters, metrics, and experiment details are saved to JSON and CSV files
Automatic experiment summary generation with all relevant information
Ability to compare recent experiments and their performances
Example script demonstrates complete workflow with logging integration
lessons_learned: Comprehensive logging is essential for reproducibility and model improvement
Structured logs (JSON, CSV) enable easy analysis and comparison of experiments
Separating console output from detailed file logs improves usability
Experiment tracking helps identify which models and hyperparameters perform best

tasks.name: Framework Integration & Testing
tasks.description: Integrate all modules, test end-to-end workflow, and fix any issues
notes: Test data loading, model training, evaluation, and backtesting
Ensure modularity and extensibility

tasks.name: Documentation & Examples
tasks.description: Complete documentation and create example notebooks/scripts
notes: Update README with usage instructions
Create example notebooks showing end-to-end workflow
Document all classes and functions
